data.title: Charles Schwab: Allocating just 1%–3% of your portfolio to BTC or ETH can significantly impact the overall risk profile.
data.description: A recent study by Charles Schwab shows that even allocating just 1%–3% of a portfolio to Bitcoin (BTC) or Ethereum (ETH) can significantly impact overall risk characteristics. The study points out that both Bitcoin and Ethereum have historically experienced declines exceeding 70%, far exceeding the volatility of stocks or bonds. Therefore, even small allocations can have a noticeable impact during periods of market volatility. Schwab proposes two methods for allocating crypto assets: 1. Traditional Portfolio Theory: Allocating based on expected returns, volatility, and correlation. However, this approach suffers from significant differences in return assumptions; if expected returns are below 10%, even aggressive investors may find it difficult to sustain large allocations. 2. Risk Budgeting: Determining the proportion of crypto assets based on willingness to accept risk, shifting the focus from returns to risk tolerance. However, crypto asset volatility can still exceed expectations. Schwab emphasizes that crypto assets are highly volatile and not suitable for all investors. Investors should carefully allocate their assets based on risk tolerance, investment horizon, and familiarity with the assets, while also being aware of risks such as liquidity issues, theft, and fraud. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Expert Predicts XRP Breakout Amid Pending U.S. Legislation
data.description: Crypto analyst John Squire has highlighted the anticipation among XRP holders, who have been waiting seven years for potential significant developments in the coming three months. According to NS3.AI, Squire views the pending U.S. digital asset legislation as a possible catalyst for a breakout. Senator Cynthia Lummis has indicated that the Senate Banking Committee might vote on the Digital Asset Market CLARITY Act as soon as this week, with a full version anticipated by the end of April.

data.title: USDC Transfer: $200 Million Moved from Treasury to Unknown Wallet
data.description: A significant transfer of 200,116,698 USDC, valued at approximately $200 million, was reported by Whale Alert. According to NS3.AI, the funds were moved from the USDC Treasury to an unidentified wallet, with the destination remaining unspecified.

data.title: Trump indicated he might adjust the deadline for Iran.
data.description: According to Fox News, US President Trump stated that the 8 p.m. deadline on Tuesday evening might be adjusted if negotiations progress, but he will adhere to the original deadline for now. After the deadline, we will continue with our planned actions against Iran. (Jinshi)

data.title: The New York Fed's one-year inflation forecast for March was 3.42%, below the expected 3.5%.
data.description: The New York Fed's 1-year inflation forecast for March was 3.42%, below the expected 3.5% and the previous reading of 3.00%. (Jinshi)

data.title: New York Fed's March Inflation Expectations Slightly Below Forecast
data.description: The New York Federal Reserve reported that the one-year inflation expectation for March was 3.42%. According to Jin10, this figure came in slightly below the anticipated 3.5% and was higher than the previous value of 3.00%. The data reflects ongoing concerns about inflationary pressures in the U.S. economy.

data.title: White House Denies Intent to Use Nuclear Weapons in Iran
data.description: On April 7, the White House has refuted claims regarding the United States' intention to deploy nuclear weapons in Iran. According to BlockBeats, this statement was reported by Al Hadath, an Arab media outlet. The denial comes amid ongoing tensions and discussions surrounding U.S. foreign policy in the region.

data.title: SOL Strategies acquires Darklake Labs for $1.2 million.
data.description: Solana treasury company SOL Strategies officially announced a definitive acquisition agreement with Darklake Labs, the company behind Solana's native zero-knowledge technology, for approximately $1.2 million, with about $1 million paid in common stock. Following the acquisition, the Darklake founding team will join SOL Strategies, including former Meta/IBM engineer Vitor Py Braga, former Coinbase compliance executive Amber Hales, and ZK research lead Tiago Alves, to advance privacy technology within the Solana ecosystem. Darklake has developed Zyga, a dynamic zero-knowledge proof system for the Solana blockchain, enabling private transaction execution and preventing front-transaction and sandwich attacks. The team placed second in the Solana Radar global hackathon in the DeFi track, joined the Colosseum accelerator, maintains academic collaborations with two Brazilian universities, and is currently applying for patents.

data.title: Circle has minted an additional 1 billion USDC in the past 24 hours.
data.description: According to Lookonchain, Circle has minted an additional 1 billion USDC in the past 24 hours.

data.title: Transcend Integrates with Canton Network to Enhance Tokenized Asset Use
data.description: Transcend has announced its integration with the Canton Network, a platform that connects over 45 central counterparties (CCPs) and five triparty agents. According to NS3.AI, this development allows institutions to incorporate tokenized assets into their existing workflows without the need to restructure their operating models. The integration aims to streamline processes and enhance efficiency in asset management.

data.title: The White House denies that the United States intends to use nuclear weapons in Iran.
data.description: According to the Arab media outlet Al Hadath, the White House denied that the United States intended to use nuclear weapons in Iran. Furthermore, the Wall Street Journal reported that Iran has severed direct diplomatic relations with the United States. (Jinshi)

data.title: CME Group to Offer 24/7 Cryptocurrency Futures Trading Starting May 29
data.description: CME Group has announced that it will begin offering 24/7 trading for its cryptocurrency futures and options starting on May 29. According to NS3.AI, this move aims to provide continuous access to the crypto markets, aligning with the round-the-clock nature of digital asset trading. Additionally, CME Group plans to introduce Avalanche and Sui futures early next month, offering both standard and micro contracts for these assets. The company reported that the average daily open interest in its crypto suite is projected to reach nearly $25 billion in 2025.

data.title: WTI Crude Oil Surges 3.75%, Exceeds $117 Per Barrel
data.description: WTI crude oil experienced a significant increase of 3.75% during the day, surpassing the $117 per barrel mark. According to NS3.AI, this rise reflects ongoing market dynamics and potential shifts in supply and demand. The upward movement in oil prices is drawing attention from investors and analysts, who are closely monitoring the factors contributing to this trend.

data.title: NVIDIA's April Trading Outlook Influenced by Oil Moves and FOMC Meeting
data.description: NVIDIA's stock is currently trading at $177.64, with a setup for April framed between $184.91 on the upside and $161.35 at the neckline. According to NS3.AI, the outlook for NVIDIA is influenced by several factors, including Iran-related oil movements and the upcoming Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting scheduled for April 28-29. Additionally, the stock is affected by a negative Chaikin Money Flow (CMF), a higher put-call ratio, and compressed implied volatility, which are contributing to the current trading dynamics.

data.title: TON Foundation Partners with SCRYPT to Enhance USDT Access on Blockchain
data.description: The TON Foundation has announced its collaboration with SCRYPT as its institutional infrastructure partner to facilitate business access to USDT on the TON Blockchain. According to NS3.AI, this integration is expected to reach over 1 billion users through its connection with Telegram. The TON Foundation highlighted that the network currently supports more than 50 million wallets.

data.title: Oil prices rose after Iran closed communication channels with the US, while gold fell below $4,610 per ounce.
data.description: According to Gate data, spot gold prices fell further after Iran closed communication channels with the US, currently down nearly $40 and below $4,610 per ounce. WTI crude oil rose $4, or 3.64%, during the day. The Nasdaq and S&P 500 indices both fell by more than 1% during the day.

data.title: Bitcoin Experiences Slight Decline Below 68000 USDT
data.description: Bitcoin has fallen below the 68000 USDT mark, currently trading at 67971.75 USDT. According to Foresight News, this represents a daily decrease of 1.27%.

data.title: Bloomberg analysts: Morgan Stanley Bitcoin ETF to list tomorrow
data.description: Eric Balchunas, a senior ETF analyst at Bloomberg, wrote on the X platform that, according to the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) listing announcement, the Morgan Stanley Bitcoin Exchange Traded Fund will begin trading tomorrow (Wednesday, April 8, Eastern Time) under the ticker symbol “MBST”.

data.title: Analysts warn that Bitcoin may fall to the $54,000 range within the next five months.
data.description: Crypto market analyst Wedson stated that Bitcoin's 720-day Tactical Bull-Bear Sentiment Index has fallen into extremely bearish territory, indicating that prolonged market fear may be nearing its end. Historically, such low levels have often been accompanied by a so-called "shakeout," after which Bitcoin enters a more robust rebound phase. For example, in 2022, when this index was low, Bitcoin fell by more than 20%; in a similar situation in 2018, Bitcoin fell by about 50%. Wedson warned that Bitcoin could experience a sharp drop of approximately $15,000 over the next five months, meaning BTC could fall from its current price to the $54,000 price range, implying that the $50,000–$55,000 range is a key support level for Bitcoin. Nevertheless, recent large Bitcoin purchases by Strategy have absorbed some selling pressure, potentially limiting downside and preventing the extreme bearish scenario from fully materializing. (Cointelegrap)

data.title: Rails to Launch Mainnet and Native Stablecoin USDr on April 30
data.description: Rails announced the launch of its mainnet scheduled for April 30, alongside the release of its native stablecoin, USDr. According to NS3.AI, USDr will serve as the platform's native stablecoin, facilitating staking and fee payments.
